channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kwara South Residents Criticise Governor AbdulRazaq for Absence Amid Rising Banditry
item.description: Residents for different communities inside Kwara South don dey para well-well as dem accuse Governor AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq say im abandon dem during serious bandit wahala. Reports talk say di governo...

item.title: Increasing Number of Canadians Delay CPP Pension to Age 70
item.description: Historically, few Canadians postponed the start of their Canada Pension Plan (CPP) benefits beyond age 65. For years, the majority opted for early access, driven by the 'bird in the hand' mindset, whi...

item.title: Understanding the Roots and Persistence of Antisemitism
item.description: In this opinion piece, Sheryl Saperia reflects on the enduring presence of antisemitism throughout history and its resurgence in contemporary society. She draws parallels between the historical suffer...

item.title: Government introduces temporary fuel levy cuts to ease April price increases
item.description: South Africans will face steep increases in fuel prices from Wednesday, 1 April 2026, as global tensions and a weaker rand drive costs higher. Mineral and Petroleum Resources Minister Gwede Mantashe a...

item.title: El-Rufai Returned to ICPC Custody After Bail Hearing
item.description: Malam Nasir El-Rufai, former governor of Kaduna State, don return to the custody of the Independent Corrupt Practices and other related Commission (ICPC). Dem release am earlier on March 27, 2026, aft...

item.title: PM Shehbaz reviews fuel supply chain, orders measures against smuggling and hoarding
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Tuesday directed tough measures to curb the smuggling and illegal hoarding of petroleum products amid ongoing global fuel supply disruptions caused by the Middle East ...

item.title: Balancing KiwiSaver contributions with household affordability
item.description: KiwiSaver, New Zealand’s retirement savings scheme, is set to increase default contributions from 3% to 3.5% for both employees and employers starting April 1, with a further rise to 4% planned for 20...

item.title: Rajnath Singh criticises Congress for neglect of Assam, urges voters to support BJP
item.description: Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Tuesday strongly criticised the Congress party for what he described as decades of neglect towards Assam, urging voters not to forgive the party in the upcoming...

item.title: DCI Investigation Finds Negligence, Not Murder, in Kericho Mass Grave
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has clarified that the discovery of human remains in a mass grave in Kericho is not linked to murder. According to DCI Director Mohamed Amin, the inves...

item.title: Key Concerns of Coimbatore South Voters Ahead of Assembly Elections
item.description: Coimbatore South Assembly constituency, which will witness a four-cornered contest on April 23, includes major commercial hubs and prime residential areas of Coimbatore city, spread across ten Corpora...

item.title: President Marcos orders official Philippine names for 131 Kalayaan Island features
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. signed Executive Order No. 111 on March 26, 2026, directing the use of official Philippine names for 131 features in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) in Palawan and the W...

item.title: Pakistan and China Issue Five-Point Peace Plan to Calm Middle East Conflict
item.description: Pakistan and China have jointly announced a five-point initiative aimed at restoring peace and stability in the Middle East amid the US-Israeli military offensive on Iran. The declaration came after D...

item.title: Durban High Court upholds defamation ruling against Calvin Mathibeli
item.description: The Durban High Court has confirmed its February ruling in the defamation case involving Calvin Mathibeli and KwaZulu-Natal police commissioner Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi. Mathibeli, a security company...

item.title: Twenty-six Nigerian lawmakers defect to new parties ahead of elections
item.description: Today, the House of Representatives experience major political shake-up as 26 lawmakers officially defect from their original parties. Lawmakers from APC, PDP, Labour Party, NNPP, and other parties mo...

item.title: Tottenham’s high‑stakes De Zerbi appointment in a relegation crisis
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur’s impending appointment of Roberto De Zerbi is portrayed as a high‑risk, high‑reward gamble in the midst of a deep crisis. The Italian manager is far from a safe, short‑term option; ...

item.title: Trump Considers Ending US Military Campaign in Iran Despite Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: According to reports, US President Donald Trump has indicated he may conclude the US military campaign in Iran even if the strategically crucial Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Administration officia...

item.title: Considerations for Helping Adult Children with Housing Without Jeopardising Your Retirement
item.description: A 74-year-old single parent with a mortgage and around $1 million in home equity seeks advice on whether to sell their home to assist three adult children following broken marriages. One child is fina...

item.title: Severe Weather Highlights Weaknesses in South Africa's Disaster Preparedness
item.description: South Africa is facing increasingly frequent and severe floods and droughts, highlighting significant gaps in disaster preparedness and response. Despite the government declaring a national state of d...

item.title: Improving maternal health services in Nigeria through targeted regional strategies
item.description: Nigeria has one of the highest maternal mortality rates globally, with over 1,000 women dying per 100,000 live births. The problem is not uniform across the country, with northern regions experiencing...

item.title: Senegal enacts law increasing prison terms for same-sex relationships
item.description: Senegal President Bassirou Diomaye Faye don sign new law wey go double maximum punishment for same-sex relations, with offenders now fit face up to 10 years in prison. The law, wey the National Assemb...
